name: Nightwatch patrol 2026-06-23: duplication + logic bug fixes

sections.context: Automated nightwatch patrol on 2026-06-23 identified: (a) one logic bug in auto-plan-run dispatch that could silently skip a newly-created plan when an agent replaces an old plan in the same run (workspacePlanIds.size <= baselinePlanIds.size early-exit is wrong), and (b) widespread code duplication across the server and CLI â€” formatError defined 22 times as private functions, GitHub REST helpers (buildHeaders/readText/readJson) duplicated between pr-annotate.ts and pr-checks.ts, env-parsing utilities (parseEnvDuration/parseEnvPositiveInt/isTruthy) duplicated between preview/eviction/config.ts and runs/reap/gc.ts, identical defaultGitCredentialsRun/defaultGitIdentityRun implementations in two supervisor files, repeated read-yaml-validate pattern inside warren-config/load.ts, resolveTargetDir duplicated between two CLI commands, and test harness infrastructure duplicated between projects.test-helpers.ts and runs.test-helpers.ts.
sections.behavior_invariant: All existing tests must continue to pass without modification. The auto-plan-run fix must not change behavior when baseline and workspace differ only in size (new-plan-added or no-change scenarios). Refactored utility references must resolve to the same logic they replaced â€” no behavior changes, only consolidation of call sites.
sections.approach: Fix the auto-plan-run bug first (logic correctness, standalone, minimal risk). Then consolidate formatError into a shared utility. Then unify the duplicated GitHub REST helpers by importing from pr-checks.ts. Then consolidate env-parsing utilities (parseEnvDuration / parseEnvPositiveInt / isTruthy) shared between preview-eviction and gc. Then deduplicate supervisor git-runner utility. Then extract the internal read-yaml-validate helper in warren-config/load.ts. Then extract resolveTargetDir CLI utility. Finally consolidate the test harness boilerplate. Each step is a single coherent PR.

steps.title: Fix auto-plan-run early-exit: replace size comparison with new-plan detection
Consolidate formatError into a shared core utility
steps.description:
The function `function formatError(err: unknown): string` is defined identically (or near-identically) as a private helper in at least 22 source files across src/seeds-cli/, src/server-config/, src/cli/, src/warren-config/, src/registry/, src/plan-runs/, src/triggers/, src/projects/, src/plot-plan-runs/, src/runs/, src/plots/.

Fix:
1. Add `export function formatError(err: unknown): string` to `src/core/errors.ts` (already the error hierarchy home).
2. Delete all 22 private `formatError` definitions.
3. Replace each call site with an import from `../../core/errors.ts` (path varies by depth).
4. Verify `bun run lint && bun run typecheck && bun test` pass.

Note: `src/cli/output.ts` already exports one. That export should be replaced by a re-export from core so external callers don't shift.

The UI's `src/ui/src/lib/format-error.ts` is separate (different package, different context) â€” leave it untouched.

steps.title: Remove duplicated GitHub REST helpers from pr-annotate.ts
steps.description:
File: src/runs/pr-annotate.ts, lines ~200-228.

`buildHeaders`, `readJson`, `readText`, and `truncate` are defined privately in pr-annotate.ts with identical bodies to the exported versions in src/runs/pr-checks.ts.

Fix: delete the four private definitions in pr-annotate.ts and import them from `./pr-checks.ts` instead. No behavior change â€” the implementations are byte-identical.

Additionally, src/ci-fixer/check-runs.ts has its own private `buildHeaders` (missing `content-type`) and `readText`. These are GET-only calls so the missing `content-type` is benign, but the `readText` implementation is identical to pr-checks.ts. Import `readText` from `../runs/pr-checks.ts` in check-runs.ts (adjust path as needed). Leave check-runs.ts's private `buildHeaders` in place since it deliberately omits `content-type` for GET-only endpoints (document this with a one-line comment if not already present).

steps.title: Consolidate env-parsing helpers shared between preview-eviction and gc
steps.description:
Files: src/preview/eviction/config.ts and src/runs/reap/gc.ts.

Both files define private `parseEnvDuration`, `parseEnvPositiveInt`, and `isTruthy` with identical implementations. `src/server/main/utils.ts` already exports `parseTrueEnv` which is identical to `isTruthy`.

Fix:
1. Extract `parseEnvDuration` and `parseEnvPositiveInt` into a shared internal module. The best home is a new file `src/server/main/env-parse.ts` (or add to `src/server/main/utils.ts` if it doesn't blow the size budget).
2. In `preview/eviction/config.ts`: delete the three local helpers, import `parseEnvDuration` and `parseEnvPositiveInt` from the shared location, replace `isTruthy` calls with `parseTrueEnv` from `src/server/main/utils.ts`.
3. In `runs/reap/gc.ts`: same substitutions.
4. Verify tests pass.

steps.title: Deduplicate supervisor git-runner utility
steps.description:
Files: src/supervisor/git-credentials.ts and src/supervisor/git-identity.ts.

`defaultGitCredentialsRun` (lines 84-97) and `defaultGitIdentityRun` (lines 110-123) have byte-identical implementations â€” both wrap `execFileAsync` with the same error-shape extraction pattern.

Fix: extract a shared `makeDefaultGitRun` factory in a new file `src/supervisor/git-run.ts` (or add to a supervisor `utils.ts`). The factory takes no arguments and returns a function matching the shared `(cmd, args) => Promise<{exitCode, stdout, stderr}>` shape. Both `git-credentials.ts` and `git-identity.ts` call `makeDefaultGitRun()` to obtain their respective `default*Run` constant.

steps.title: Extract read-yaml-validate helper inside warren-config/load.ts
steps.description:
File: src/warren-config/load.ts, lines 227-256 and 330-359.

The read-file â†’ trim â†’ YAML-parse â†’ validate-against-schema pattern is duplicated twice in the same file. The only difference between the two blocks is the validator function called at the end (`parseConfigFile` vs `parsePreviewFile`).

Fix: extract a private `loadYamlFile<T>(input: LoadInput, absPath: string, relPath: string, parse: (doc: unknown) => ParseResult<T>): Promise<T | null>` helper. Replace both duplicated blocks with calls to this helper. No behavior change.

steps.title: Extract resolveTargetDir CLI helper shared between config-migrate and init
steps.description:
Files: src/cli/commands/config-migrate.ts and src/cli/commands/init.ts.

`resolveTargetDir` is defined with an identical body in both files (lines 169-192 in config-migrate.ts, lines 159-183 in init.ts). The only difference is the `deps` type (ConfigMigrateDeps vs InitDeps), but both satisfy the same structural requirement: `deps.projects.get(id): Promise<{localPath: string} | null>`.

Fix: extract `resolveTargetDir` into a shared `src/cli/commands/shared.ts` (or add to an existing shared helpers module if one exists). Use a structural interface for `deps` so both callers can pass their own `deps` type without a common base class.

steps.title: Consolidate server handler test harness boilerplate
steps.description:
Files: src/server/handlers/projects.test-helpers.ts and src/server/handlers/runs.test-helpers.ts.

These two files share 135 lines of identical test infrastructure (poolFor, silentLogger, stub, BurrowFixture interface, and makeBurrowClient). This means any change to the shared stubs must be applied twice.

Fix: create `src/server/handlers/test-helpers.ts` (or `src/server/test-helpers.ts`) that exports the shared helpers. Update `projects.test-helpers.ts` and `runs.test-helpers.ts` to import and re-export from the new shared module rather than defining duplicates. Tests that already import from these files will continue to work without changes.

Also consolidate the overlapping blocks between plots.test-support.ts and plots.workbench.harness.ts (3 separate clone blocks: lines 56-70, 82-102, 177-213 in test-support.ts matching blocks in workbench.harness.ts) using the same shared-and-re-export pattern.

acceptance: bun test passes with no failures after each step
For step 1: new unit test covering baseline=[pl-old] workspace=[pl-new] scenario passes and returns autoPlanRunCreated=true
For steps 2-8: no import from a local private formatError/buildHeaders/etc. definition where a shared export is now available
